name: Times Tables — Patterns, Not Parroting
whatIsIt: A times table is just skip-counting written down — and every table hides beautiful PATTERNS that make it easy: the 2s are all even, the 5s end in 5 or 0, the 9s' digits add to 9, the 10s just wear a zero. Learn the patterns and half the table learns itself!
whyNeeded: Tables are the multiplication toolbox you'll reach for every single day — bills, big multiplications, division's twin trick. Parroting is boring and breaks under pressure; PATTERNS stick forever and rebuild any fact you forget.
realLifeUses: Quick shop math: 4 pens at ₹5 each — table of 5!
Cricket: 6 balls per over — the table of 6 counts balls
Time: 5 minutes per clock number — the 5s again!
Sharing and packing — division uses the same tables backwards

vocabulary.meaning: A list of one number's multiples: 4, 8, 12, 16… is the table of 4.
A number in a table: 12 is a multiple of 4 (and of 3, 6, 2, 12!).
A shortcut hiding in a table, like '9s digits add to 9'.

story.title: The Nine-Times Finger Secret
story.text: Meena dreads the nine-times table. Her teacher shows a clever trick: 'Hold up all ten fingers. For 9 × 4, fold down your 4th finger. Count the fingers to its LEFT — 3. Count the fingers to its RIGHT — 6. That is 36! And look: 3 + 6 = 9, every single time. A table is full of patterns, not just chanting.'
story.extractedProblem: Find 9 × 4 using the digit pattern or the finger trick.
story.answerInStory: 36 — fold the 4th finger: 3 on the left, 6 on the right. And 3+6 = 9, the 9s' secret!

visual.caption: A table is skip-counting: jump 9, 18, 27, 36... The patterns live in the landing numbers!

teachingGallery.title: Tables are patterns, not parroting
teachingGallery.note: Every table has a rhythm that makes it stick.

standardMethod.summary: Build tables from skip-counting + patterns + the facts you already own.
steps: Spy the pattern: 2s all even; 5s end 5/0; 9s digits add to 9; 10s add a zero.
Use turn-around facts: 7×4 = 4×7 — every fact you know buys you its twin!
Stuck on one? Build from a neighbour: 6×7 = 5×7 + one more 7 = 35+7 = 42.

alternateMethods.name: The 9s finger trick
steps: For 9 × n, fold the n-th finger from the left.
Fingers LEFT of the fold = tens; RIGHT = ones.

alternateMethods.name: Neighbour-fact building
steps: Start from an easy neighbour (×5 or ×10).

workedExamples.problem: Ravi forgot 6 × 8. Build it from a neighbour fact he knows: 5 × 8 = 40.
steps: 6 groups of 8 is just 5 groups of 8… plus ONE more 8.
6 × 8 = 48 — no parroting needed, just a neighbour and a hop.

workedExamples.problem: Is 73 in the table of 5? Decide WITHOUT skip-counting all the way.
steps: Pattern rule: every multiple of 5 ends in 5 or 0.
So 73 is NOT a multiple of 5 — the pattern answers instantly!
workedExamples.answer: No — multiples of 5 end in 5 or 0.

trickPractice.intro: Your two hands are a 9× calculator — fold one finger and read the answer.

hintLadder: Hold up all 10 fingers. Fold down finger number 2.
Fingers LEFT of the fold = tens = 1. Fingers RIGHT = ones = 8.
Hold up all 10 fingers. Fold down finger number 3.
Fingers LEFT of the fold = tens = 2. Fingers RIGHT = ones = 7.
Hold up all 10 fingers. Fold down finger number 4.
Fingers LEFT of the fold = tens = 3. Fingers RIGHT = ones = 6.
Hold up all 10 fingers. Fold down finger number 5.
Fingers LEFT of the fold = tens = 4. Fingers RIGHT = ones = 5.
Hold up all 10 fingers. Fold down finger number 6.
Fingers LEFT of the fold = tens = 5. Fingers RIGHT = ones = 4.
Hold up all 10 fingers. Fold down finger number 7.
Fingers LEFT of the fold = tens = 6. Fingers RIGHT = ones = 3.
Hold up all 10 fingers. Fold down finger number 8.
Fingers LEFT of the fold = tens = 7. Fingers RIGHT = ones = 2.
Hold up all 10 fingers. Fold down finger number 9.
Fingers LEFT of the fold = tens = 8. Fingers RIGHT = ones = 1.

commonMistakes.mistake: Memorising the chant only — then one forgotten line crashes the whole table.
commonMistakes.fix: Own the REBUILD tools: skip-count up, use a neighbour fact (6×7 = 5×7+7), or flip it (7×6). A pattern-owner can always rebuild; a parrot can only restart the chant.

commonMistakes.mistake: Knowing 4×7 but freezing at 7×4 as if it's a new fact.
commonMistakes.fix: Turn-around twins! The array flipped sideways is the same array: a×b = b×a, always. Every fact you learn comes with a free twin.

commonMistakes.mistake: Stretching a pattern too far — like 'all multiples of 9 have digits adding to 9, so 89 must be one'.
commonMistakes.fix: Patterns point, checks prove! 8+9 = 17, not 9 — and 89 isn't in the 9s (81, 90 skip it). Use the pattern to filter, then verify with a quick skip-count or neighbour fact.

easy.q: Which of these is a multiple of 5?
hintLadder: The 5s pattern: how do they all end?

medium.q: Use the finger trick (or digit pattern): 9 × 6 = ?

challenge.q: The hard one — 7 × 8. Build it: 7 × 10 = 70, now remove two 7s.
Ravi announces: '89's digits are big so it's in the 9 table!' Test him properly.

options.label: He's right — big digits mean big tables
Can't tell without the full chant

hintLadder: Not 9 — the pattern rejects it, and skip-counting confirms.

options.label: A totally new fact to memorise
A number with a 2 in it

hintLadder: Skip-count the 2s and watch the endings.
The last digit is always even.

teachBackPrompt: You're a pattern spy now! Teach someone at home the 9s finger trick AND one neighbour-build (like 6×7 from 5×7). Show them why you never need to panic about a forgotten fact.

revisionCard.summary: Tables = skip-counting + patterns: 2s even, 5s end 5/0, 9s digits sum to 9 (finger trick!), 10s add a zero. Turn-around twins (4×7 = 7×4). Forgot one? Neighbour-build: 6×7 = 5×7+7 = 42. Patterns point, checks prove!

gameMission.title: The Pattern Spy Academy
gameMission.brief: Multi's orchard rows hide secret table patterns! Crack the 5s gate, the 9s vault, and the turn-around twin doors — and rebuild the facts Ravi's parroting crashed.

realLifeProject: Pattern hunt: write the 9 table down one side of a page and circle the tens and ones separately. Show a family member the up-and-down staircase pattern, then wow them with the finger trick.
